eo.t: [计] 基本操作, 允许输出, 操作结束, 执行命令

eo ipso.t: [拉丁语]；即因此(而)…；这就是说…；这就等于…, 这样, 因而, 从而

eo-.t: [医]黎明, 曙, 最初, 原始

eobiogenesis.t: 原始生命起源, 曙生物发生

eobiont.t: n. [生]原生物(生命起源中的一个假想阶段)

eobs.t: abbr. electro-optical beam splitter 电光分束器

eoc.t: [计] 代码结束, 转换结束

eocene.t:
n. 始新世, 始新系
a. 始新世的, 始新系的
eocene.d: n. from 58 million to 40 million years ago; presence of modern mammals

eoct.t: abbr. electro-optical countermeasures technology 电光对抗技术

eod.t: abbr. every other day 每隔一天; explosive ordnance demolition 爆炸性军火破坏

eodosteoma.t: [医] 骨髓腔肿瘤, 内生骨瘤, 中心性骨瘤

eoe.t: abbr. 机会均等雇主（equal opportunity employer）；射出地球轨道（earth orbit ejection）；错误遗漏不在此限（Errors and Omissions Excepted）；欧洲期权交易（European Options Exchange）

eogene.t: (=Paleogene)[地质]早第三纪, 下第三纪

eogenetic.t: 近表成的, 早期生的

eohippus.d: n. earliest horse; extinct primitive dog-sized four-toed Eocene animal

eoka.t: n. [史]埃奥卡( 希腊语" 为塞浦路斯而斗争全国组织")

eolande.t: 约兰德(女子名, 亦作 Yolande, Yolanda)

eolian.t: a. [希神]风神 Aeolus 的, 伊奥利亚人的, 风的, 风蚀的
eolian.d: n a member of one of four linguistic divisions of the prehistoric Greeks

eolic.t:
a. 伊欧里斯的,伊欧里斯人的
n. 伊欧里斯语
eolic.d: n the dialect of Ancient Greek spoken in Thessaly and Boeotia and Aeolis

eolienne.t: n. [纺]风神绸(丝毛横棱纹薄绸)

eolith.d: n. a crude stone artifact (as a chipped flint); possibly the earliest tools

eolithic.d: a. of or relating to the earliest period of the Stone Age (characterized by the use of eoliths)

eolomotion.t: 缓下风移动, 缓下坡蠕动

eolotropic.d: s having properties with different values along different axes

eomatist.t: [医] 躯体论者(躯体论认为一切精神病及神经机能病均由躯体病变所致)

eon.d:
n. the longest division of geological time
n. an immeasurably long period of time
n. (Gnosticism) a divine power or nature emanating from the Supreme Being and playing various roles in the operation of the universe

eonian.t: a. 永远的, 永久的
eonian.d:
a of or relating to a geological eon (longer than an era)
s continuing forever or indefinitely

eons.d:
n the longest division of geological time
n an immeasurably long period of time
n (Gnosticism) a divine power or nature emanating from the Supreme Being and playing various roles in the operation of the universe

eop.t: abbr. earth orbit plane 地球轨道平面; end of part 部分结束; end of passage 通道终点

eophyte.t: n. (岩石中的)始生代植物化石
eophyte.d:
n. A fossil plant which is found in the lowest beds of the
   Silurian age.

eophytic.d: a. Of or pertaining to eophytes.

eops.t: (eop 的复数) abbr. earth orbit plane 地球轨道平面; end of part 部分结束; end of passage 通道终点

eoq.t: abbr. economical ordering quantity 经济订购量，经济批量; end of quarter 季度末

eora.t: abbr. elderly-onset rheumatoid arthritis 老年发病类风湿性关节炎; explosive ordnance reconnaisance agent 爆炸物侦察员（美国陆军）

eos.t:
n. 黎明女神
[计] 屏幕结束, 段结束, 步结束, 序列结束, 可扩充的操作系统
eos.d: n. (Greek mythology) the winged goddess of the dawn in ancient mythology; daughter of Hyperion; identified with Roman Aurora

eosin.t:
n. 曙红色染料
[化] 曙红; 酸性曙红; 四溴荧光素
eosin.d: n. a red fluorescent dye resulting from the action of bromine on fluorescein; used in cosmetics and as a biological stain for studying cell structures

eosin-methylthionine chloride culture-me.t: [医] 曙红美蓝培养基, 曙红美蓝琼脂

eosine.t:
n. 曙红
[化] 曙红; 酸性曙红; 四溴荧光素

eosinopenia.d: n a decrease in the number of eosinophils in the blood

eosinopenic reaction.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞减少反应

eosinophil.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞, 嗜酸性细胞
eosinophil.d: n. a leukocyte readily stained with eosin

eosinophil cells.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞, 嗜酸性细胞

eosinophil leukocyte.t: [医] 嗜曙红白细胞, 嗜酸性白细胞

eosinophile.d: n a leukocyte readily stained with eosin

eosinophile granulocyte.t: [医] 嗜曙红粒细胞, 嗜曙红粒性白细胞

eosinophilia.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞增多, 嗜曙红性
eosinophilia.d: n. a symptom of allergic states; increased eosinophils in the blood

eosinophilia infectiosa.t: [医] 感染性嗜曙红细胞增多

eosinophilia-hepatomegaly syndrome.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞增多性肝肥大综合征

eosinophilic.t: [医] 嗜曙红的, 嗜酸性的
eosinophilic.d: a. of or relating to eosinophil

eosinophilic pneumonopathy.t: [医] 嗜曙红细胞性肺病, 吕弗勒氏综合征

eosinophilous.t: 嗜曙红的, 嗜酸性的

eosinophils.t: (eosinophil 的复数) [医] 嗜曙红细胞, 嗜酸性细胞
eosinophils.d:
plural of eosinophil
n. a leukocyte readily stained with eosin

eosote.t: [医] 缬草酸木溜油酯, 戊酸木溜油酯

eosphorite.d:
n. A hydrous phosphate of alumina and manganese. It is
   generally of a rose-pink color, -- whence the name.

eot.t: [计] 带结束, 任务结束, 测试结束, 文本结束, 传输结束

eozoic.t:
n. 始生代
a. 始生代的
eozoic.d:
a. Of or pertaining to rocks or strata older than the
   Paleozoic, in many of which the eozoon has been found.

eozoon.d:
n. A peculiar structure found in the Archaean limestones of
   Canada and other regions. By some geologists it is believed to be a
   species of gigantic Foraminifera, but others consider it a concretion,
   without organic structure.

eozoonal.d:
a. Pertaining to the eozoon; containing eozoons; as,
   eozoonal limestone.

eozoons.t: n. [地质] 始生物；曙动物
